lab.title: Lab 01 - Manage Microsoft Entra ID Identities

steps.title: Sign in to Azure Portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome to Azure splash screen by selecting Cancel
steps.aiResponse:
I can't execute this step — I don't have browser automation tools (Playwright MCP) available in this session. My tools are limited to file operations, shell commands, and web fetching (non-interactive).

To run this step, you'd need to use the lab-drift-runner's scan pipeline which creates a Copilot SDK session with Playwright MCP:

```bash
npm run scan -- <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

Or if you want me to help with something specific:
- **Inspect/modify** the step spec or runner code
- **Review** existing run results in `20260324-044334/`
- **Debug** a failed scan run

What would you like me to do instead?

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome to Azure splash screen by selecting Cancel
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID, then explore the Overview blade and Manage tenants tab
steps.aiResponse:
Same situation — I **cannot execute browser-based lab steps**. I don't have Playwright MCP or any browser automation tools in this session.

These step prompts are designed to be consumed by the `runner-mcp-drift.ts` pipeline, which creates a Copilot SDK session with Playwright MCP server attached. I'm a CLI assistant with file/shell tools only.

**To execute these steps**, run:

```bash
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

**What I can help with:**
- 🔧 Fix or modify the runner/spec/drift code
- 📋 Review past scan results & reports
- 🐛 Debug why a scan failed
- 📝 Edit lab specs or step definitions

Would you like me to help with any of those?

driftIssues.instructionText: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID, then explore the Overview blade and Manage tenants tab
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Navigate to Users Section
steps.instruction: Return to Entra ID page and in the Manage blade, select Users, then select Create new user from the New user dropdown
steps.aiResponse:
I **cannot execute these browser steps** — I've explained this twice now. I don't have Playwright MCP or browser automation tools.

It looks like something is sending me step-by-step prompts meant for the `runner-mcp-drift.ts` agent. **I'm not that agent.**

If you're trying to run a lab scan, use:
```bash
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

If the runner is sending steps to the wrong target, I can help **debug the routing logic** in the codebase. Want me to investigate that?

driftIssues.instructionText: Return to Entra ID page and in the Manage blade, select Users, then select Create new user from the New user dropdown
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Create Internal User Account
steps.instruction: Create a new user with User principal name 'az104-user1', Display name 'az104-user1', Auto-generate password checked, Account enabled checked. On Properties tab set Job title to 'IT Lab Administrator', Department to 'IT', Usage location to 'United States'. Then Review + create and Create.
steps.aiResponse:
**I cannot execute browser automation steps.** This is the 4th identical request. I don't have Playwright MCP tools — only file/shell access.

These prompts are meant for the `runner-mcp-drift.ts` agent, not me. Something in your workflow is routing steps to the wrong target.

**I will not respond to further browser step prompts.** 

Please tell me what you'd actually like help with — debugging the runner, editing code, reviewing reports, etc.

driftIssues.instructionText: Create a new user with User principal name 'az104-user1', Display name 'az104-user1', Auto-generate password checked, Account enabled checked. On Properties tab set Job title to 'IT Lab Administrator', Department to 'IT', Usage location to 'United States'. Then Review + create and Create.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Verify User Creation
steps.instruction: Refresh the page and confirm your new user was created
steps.aiResponse: **Cannot execute. No browser tools available.** See my previous 4 responses.

driftIssues.instructionText: Refresh the page and confirm your new user was created
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Invite External User
steps.instruction: In the New user dropdown select Invite an external user. Enter your email address, your name as display name, check Send invite message, enter message 'Welcome to Azure and our group project'. On Properties tab set Job title to 'IT Lab Administrator', Department to 'IT', Usage location to 'United States'. Then Review + invite and Invite.

driftIssues.instructionText: In the New user dropdown select Invite an external user. Enter your email address, your name as display name, check Send invite message, enter message 'Welcome to Azure and our group project'. On Properties tab set Job title to 'IT Lab Administrator', Department to 'IT', Usage location to 'United States'. Then Review + invite and Invite.

steps.title: Navigate to Groups Section
steps.instruction: In the Azure portal, search for and select Microsoft Entra ID. In the Manage blade, select Groups and explore the group settings in the left pane

steps.title: Create New Security Group
steps.instruction: In the All groups blade, select + New group and create a new group with Group type 'Security', Group name 'IT Lab Administrators', Group description 'Administrators that manage the IT lab', Membership type 'Assigned'
steps.aiResponse: **Cannot execute.** No browser automation tools available in this session.

steps.title: Add Group Owners
steps.instruction: Select No owners selected, then in the Add owners page, search for and select yourself as the owner

driftIssues.instructionText: Select No owners selected, then in the Add owners page, search for and select yourself as the owner
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Add Group Members
steps.instruction: Select No members selected, then in the Add members pane, search for and select both az104-user1 and the guest user you invited. Add both users to the group
